purpose: Hydrogel Phase 2 AF3-Multimer validation of top-3 SAP candidates from Phase 1 analytical pre-screen. Tests both dual-interface binding handles (TMEM145 + actin) at realistic peptide length, converting Phase 1 Kd=100 nM assumption from analytical hand-wave to structural evidence.

source_sequences.TMEM145_full: verified from prior AF3 batches, 493 aa
source_sequences.ACTG1: UniProt P63261, cytoplasmic gamma-1 actin, 375 aa, main stereocilia isoform (fetched 2026-04-23)

hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_tmem145.description: Peptide rada16_wh2_native (45 aa) + TMEM145 full (493 aa) = 538 aa. Tests whether the TMEM145-binding tail (derived from STRC aa 1669-1680 contact cluster) retains recognition when fused to the RADA16/EAK16 self-assembling scaffold plus WH2 actin-binder.
hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_tmem145.success_criteria: ipTM >= 0.50 (Phase 2 gate, tighter than Phase 1 0.40 baseline). Peptide C-terminal tail residues (last ~12 aa, positions 34-45) drive the interface. TMEM145 contact residues cluster in the same zone as native STRC contacts (cross-referenced against [[STRC Ultra-Mini Full-Length TMEM145 AF3]] GOLD zone).
hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_tmem145.hypothesis_impact: pass -> TMEM145-binding handle is functional at real peptide length; one of two interfaces validated. fail -> short-peptide Kd assumption (100 nM in Phase 1 Gate 3) falsified; Gate 3 f=0.70 estimate collapses; revisit design (longer tail, structured helical mimetic, or alternative TMEM145 epitope from STRC GOLD zone).

hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_actin.description: Peptide rada16_wh2_native (45 aa) + G-actin trimer (3 x 375 aa ACTG1) = 1170 aa. Tests whether the WH2 motif N-terminus retains actin binding when fused to the self-assembling scaffold. G-actin trimer captures near-filament geometry without the full F-actin AF3 context cost.
hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_actin.success_criteria: ipTM >= 0.50 on at least one peptide-actin pair. WH2 motif (peptide positions 1-17) engages the actin barbed-end cleft (actin subdomains 1 and 3, residues around D1/T148/D286).
hydrogel_rada16_wh2_native_x_actin.hypothesis_impact: pass -> actin-binding handle functional; one of two interfaces validated. fail -> WH2 fusion to RADA16/EAK16 scaffold disrupts actin recognition; peptide cannot localize to stereocilia cores; hypothesis architecturally broken unless an alternative actin-binder (espin mini, fascin, filamin-derived peptide) is engineered in.

how_to_submit: AlphaFold Server (alphafoldserver.com): Import from JSON for each file. Daily limit 20; this batch uses 6. Seed 42 for consistency with 2026-04-22 and 2026-04-23 STRC batches.

decision_rules.both_interfaces_pass_for_any_candidate: Hypothesis #9 [[STRC Synthetic Peptide Hydrogel HTC]]: B -> A (evidence depth +2). Advance to Phase 2b Martini3 MD dose-response sweep and Phase 2c wet-lab cell-level actin-bundling assay.
decision_rules.tmem145_pass_actin_fail: B stays B. TMEM145 handle works, but the actin-bundling architecture is broken. Redesign N-terminus before Phase 2b.
decision_rules.tmem145_fail_actin_pass: B stays B. Short peptide TMEM145 Kd is the real gate (Phase 1 Gate 3 sensitivity confirmed). Retool tail.
decision_rules.both_fail: B -> C. Dual-interface short-peptide architecture is not structurally competent at AF3 confidence; hypothesis needs structured-mimetic approach (miniprotein, cyclic peptide) not SAP.
